claims.claim_text: A retail company's recommendation system looked healthy on Friday.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, opening anecdote
claims.anchor_text: A retail company's recommendation system looked healthy on Friday.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A recommendation system can appear healthy before a hidden data problem shows up in production.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed anecdote with a fabricated-looking timeline, so the named scenario should be generalized.

claims.claim_text: By Monday morning, revenue had dropped because the model had learned from a partial export that contained only one region's weekend data.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, opening anecdote
claims.anchor_text: By Monday morning, revenue had dropped because the model had learned from a partial export that contained only one region's weekend data.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A model trained on a partial or unrepresentative export can hurt business metrics after deployment.
claims.rationale: This preserves the lesson while removing uncited timeline and causal specifics from a composite story.

claims.span_hint: section: 2. Choosing the Right Orchestrator, War Story: The 3AM Pager
claims.suggested_rewrite: Data ingestion ran first in the overnight workflow.
claims.rationale: The exact hour is uncited incident detail and should be removed.

claims.span_hint: section: 2. Choosing the Right Orchestrator, War Story: The 3AM Pager
claims.rationale: The exact hour is uncited incident detail and should be removed.

claims.span_hint: section: 2. Choosing the Right Orchestrator, War Story: The 3AM Pager
claims.suggested_rewrite: Deployment ran later in the workflow.
claims.rationale: The exact hour is uncited incident detail and should be removed.

claims.span_hint: section: 2. Choosing the Right Orchestrator, War Story: The 3AM Pager
claims.suggested_rewrite: A sharp production-metric drop triggered an overnight page.
claims.rationale: The precise time and percentage are uncited incident specifics and must be removed.

claims.claim_text: Prefect and Dagster fit Python-first teams that want testable workflow code and modern developer experience.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Choosing the Right Orchestrator, Prefect / Dagster paragraph
claims.anchor_text: Prefect and Dagster fit Python-first teams that want testable workflow code and modern developer experience.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Python-native orchestrators can be attractive to teams that prefer workflow code and strong local testing ergonomics.
claims.rationale: This is a multi-vendor evaluative claim that is too subjective to support cleanly from one primary source.

claims.claim_text: Kubeflow Pipelines components are Python functions wrapped with the `@component` decorator.
claims.rationale: The KFP DSL reference directly documents `kfp.dsl.component` as the decorator for Python-function-based components.

claims.claim_text: The compile step converts Python pipeline structure into a pipeline package/YAML artifact.
claims.span_hint: section: 3. Building Kubeflow Pipelines Correctly, Compiling and Running
claims.rationale: The KFP compiler reference explicitly states that `Compiler().compile(...)` compiles a pipeline function into workflow YAML.

claims.claim_text: KFP `Client.create_run_from_pipeline_func` and `create_run_from_pipeline_package` submit runs to a KFP-enabled Kubernetes cluster.
claims.span_hint: section: 3. Building Kubeflow Pipelines Correctly, Compiling and Running
claims.rationale: The KFP client reference directly documents both methods as running pipelines on a KFP-enabled Kubernetes cluster.

claims.claim_text: A line such as `uses: @scripts/pipeline_v4_batch.py` would be invalid because `uses:` expects an action reference, not a local Python script path.
claims.span_hint: section: 5. CI/CD for ML, GitHub Actions for ML
claims.rationale: GitHub's workflow-syntax docs directly define `steps[*].uses` and `run`, but `docs.github.com` is off the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Apache Airflow models workflows as DAGs with schedules, tasks, and task dependencies.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Choosing the Right Orchestrator, Airflow paragraph
claims.rationale: The Airflow DAGs reference explicitly describes schedules, tasks, and task dependencies as core DAG attributes.

claims.claim_text: Airflow has a very extensive set of operators, including built-in and provider-packaged operators.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Choosing the Right Orchestrator, Airflow paragraph
claims.rationale: The Airflow operators reference directly states that Airflow has a very extensive set of operators, including community provider packages.

claims.claim_text: Argo Workflows is a Kubernetes-native workflow engine supporting DAG and step-based workflows.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Choosing the Right Orchestrator, Argo Workflows paragraph
claims.rationale: The Argo Workflows project page states this capability directly.

claims.claim_text: For containers in a Pod, Kubernetes uses resource requests for scheduling and enforces resource limits at runtime.
claims.span_hint: section: 6. Pipeline Reliability Patterns, Resource Management
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes Pods documentation explicitly says the scheduler uses requests for placement and the kubelet enforces limits.

further_reading.title: Kubeflow Pipelines DSL API Reference
further_reading.why_relevant: Canonical reference for `@component`, `dsl.If`, retries, and task-level pipeline APIs.

further_reading.title: Apache Airflow DAGs
further_reading.why_relevant: Grounds the scheduler and DAG concepts used in the orchestrator comparison.

further_reading.title: Argo Workflows
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary overview for Argo's Kubernetes-native DAG and step workflow model.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Resource Management for Pods and Containers
further_reading.why_relevant: Direct reference for CPU, memory, and other resource request/limit behavior that affects ML pipeline reliability.

notes: No shared section pool was referenced, so all URLs are proposed as new. Code blocks, Mermaid diagrams, quiz items, and comparison tables were excluded unless the same factual claim was made in prose. GitHub Actions syntax claims require allowlist expansion because the authoritative docs live on docs.github.com.
